oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Communication: A cornerstone to clinic culture
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Berry, Brittany
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: communication
culture
KAI
Myers-Briggs
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Maintaining an effective stream of communication within your clinic is a foundational part of creating and sustaining a com­pany’s culture. This includes communication among doctors and to staff members. How one communicates in all aspects of their day determines how they are perceived by others. Is communication constant and open? Is communication short and infrequent? Is the objective comprehended and retained? At Salt Creek Veterinary Hospital we have learned that in order to maintain constant and open communication, we must be in­tentional about prioritizing it. We have implemented different strategies that focus on continually improving communication within our company. Strategies include structured communi­cations, fostering individual communication styles, and using technology to increase the efficiency of communication.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 151
